skill.name: python-pro
skill.description: Guide modern Python 3.12+ development with async patterns, type hints, testing, performance tuning, packaging, and production service practices.
skill.summary: Build, review, and optimize production Python code with modern tools and patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported one external command pattern and seven weak cryptography patterns, but review found they are false positives in instructional Markdown. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, executable script, network call, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static external command finding is the phrase "the `with` statement" inside documentation. It is Markdown inline code, not Ruby or shell backtick execution, and it does not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is a Python language concept written as inline Markdown. There is no shell command, script block, or execution instruction at this location.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Match in Front Matter
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography finding on line 3 is a substring match in the YAML word "description". The line describes the skill and does not mention a cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact context is metadata text, not implementation guidance. No weak hash, cipher, or encryption recommendation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Match in Design Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography finding on line 16 is a substring match in "Designing". The line describes Python service or tooling design, not cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: This is a natural language bullet about production-ready Python work. There is no algorithm choice, key handling, or encryption behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Match in Descriptor Topic
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography finding on line 43 is a substring match in "Descriptors". The line lists advanced Python object-oriented patterns and does not reference encryption.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Python descriptors are a language feature unrelated to cryptographic primitives. No insecure crypto usage is described at this location.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Match in Data Pipeline Topic
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography finding on line 91 is a substring match in "Data". The line discusses data pipeline and ETL design, not cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is data engineering guidance. There is no DES, MD5, SHA1, RC4, or similar weak algorithm mentioned.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Match in Design Patterns Topic
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography finding on line 107 is a substring match in "Design". The line lists software design patterns and does not discuss cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line names general software patterns such as Singleton, Factory, and Observer. No crypto implementation or insecure security advice appears there.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Match in FastAPI Example
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography finding on line 153 is a substring match in "Design". The line is an example request for FastAPI error handling and validation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The example concerns API structure, validation, and errors. It contains no cryptographic primitive or insecure security recommendation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Match in Task System Example
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography finding on line 157 is a substring match in "Design". The line is an example request for a Celery background task system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line describes application architecture for background work. It has no weak cryptography guidance or executable behavior.

content.value_statement: Modern Python projects need clear patterns for quality, performance, and production readiness. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Python 3.12+ design, testing, tooling, and deployment work.
actual_capabilities: Recommends Python 3.12+ language features and typing patterns.
Guides async workflows with asyncio, aiohttp, trio, and related libraries.
Plans modern project tooling with uv, ruff, mypy, pyright, and pytest.
Reviews FastAPI, Django, Flask, SQLAlchemy, and Pydantic application patterns.
Suggests profiling, benchmarking, caching, and memory optimization approaches.
Covers deployment topics such as Docker, CI/CD, monitoring, and configuration.
limitations: It provides guidance only and does not include executable helper scripts.
It depends on the host assistant to inspect project files and run tools.
It may need updates as Python libraries and packaging practices change.
It is focused on Python and does not cover non-Python stacks in depth.

use_cases.title: Modernize a Python Codebase
use_cases.description: Upgrade dependencies, typing, formatting, and packaging practices for a Python 3.12+ project.

use_cases.title: Design a Production API
use_cases.description: Plan FastAPI or Django services with validation, error handling, tests, and deployment guidance.

use_cases.title: Improve Python Performance
use_cases.description: Identify bottlenecks and choose async, profiling, caching, or data processing improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Review Python Basics
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small function or module.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Python code for readability, typing, and simple correctness issues. Suggest clear improvements for a beginner.

prompt_templates.title: Set Up Tooling
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate project setup with modern quality tools.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me configure a modern Python project with uv, ruff, mypy or pyright, pytest, and a clean pyproject.toml structure.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Async Service
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced service design for web or data workflows.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an async Python service architecture for this workflow. Include concurrency choices, validation, testing, and operational concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Production Performance
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced diagnosis of latency, memory, or throughput problems.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Python performance problem. Propose profiling steps, likely bottlenecks, measurable fixes, and regression tests.

output_examples.input: Review a FastAPI endpoint for production readiness.
output: A prioritized review of validation, error handling, typing, and dependency injection.
Focused testing recommendations with pytest and realistic fixtures.
Deployment notes for configuration, logging, and monitoring.

output_examples.input: Improve a slow Python data pipeline.
output: A profiling plan that separates CPU, I/O, memory, and database costs.
Optimization options such as batching, caching, vectorization, or async I/O.
Benchmark and regression checks to verify the improvement.

output_examples.input: Create a modern Python project structure.
output: A recommended package layout with tests, configuration, and documentation paths.
Tooling choices for formatting, linting, typing, dependency locking, and CI.
Guidance for migration from older pip or setup.py workflows.

best_practices: Provide runtime version, dependency constraints, and deployment context before asking for architecture guidance.
Ask for tests and validation steps with every implementation or refactor request.
Use profiling data before requesting performance changes for production systems.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for broad non-Python architecture decisions without Python-specific requirements.
Do not accept performance changes without measurable benchmarks or regression tests.
Do not request secret handling examples with real credentials or production tokens.

faq.question: Does this skill write Python code?
faq.answer: It guides the assistant to produce Python code, reviews, tests, and project plans when the host tool supports code work.

faq.question: Which Python versions does it target?
faq.answer: It focuses on Python 3.12 and newer practices while still recognizing common earlier patterns.

faq.question: Can it help with FastAPI and Django?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for FastAPI, Django, Flask, Pydantic, SQLAlchemy, and related web service patterns.

faq.question: Can it improve performance?
faq.answer: Yes. It can recommend profiling, async I/O, caching, vectorization, and benchmark-driven optimization steps.

faq.question: Does it run commands by itself?
faq.answer: No. The skill file only contains guidance. Command execution depends on the surrounding assistant environment.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: The audit found only static-analysis false positives in Markdown and no confirmed malicious behavior.
